green-light-or-arrow: Go if the way is clear
yellow-x-or-yellow-diagonal-downward-arr: Move out of this lane safely
green-arrow: You may drive in this lane
left-turn-arrow: You may turn in the arrow's direction
pedestrian-hybrid-beacons-phbs: Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
triangle-yield: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
rectangle-regulatory-or-guide: Gives the law or directional information
diamond-warning: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
pentagon-school-zone-school-crossing: School zone or school crossing ahead
speed-limit: Maximum legal speed in good conditions
do-not-enter-wrong-way: Do not drive in this direction
one-way: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
no-turn-on-red: Do not turn while the light is red
do-not-pass: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
left-turn-yield-on-green: Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
keep-right: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
lane-use-control: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
high-occupancy-vehicle: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
disabled-parking: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
advisory-speed: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
stop-ahead-yield-ahead: A stop or yield sign is ahead
signal-ahead: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
merge: Two lanes are merging into one
lane-reduction: The right lane ends ahead; merge left
divided-highway-begins: The road ahead is divided by a median
divided-highway-ends: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
slippery-when-wet: Road is slippery when wet — slow down
low-clearance-sign: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
hill: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
deer-crossing: Watch for deer crossing the road
horse-drawn-buggies: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
tractors-and-farm-equipment: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
bicycle-crossing-bike-path: Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
school-zone-school-crossing: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
open-joints: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
expansion-joints: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
y-intersection: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
t-intersection: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
right-curve-side-road: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
sharp-right-turn: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
sharp-right-and-left-turns: Sharp right then left turns ahead
right-and-left-curves: The road curves right, then left
right-curve-with-safe-speed-indicator: Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
low-ground-railroad-crossing: Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
railroad-crossbuck-and-flashing-lights: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
crossbuck-flashing-lights-and-gate: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
rough-road-bump-or-uneven-lanes: Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
flashing-arrow-boards: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
flaggers: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
traffic-control-devices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
